summary: Comprehensive sales and pricing audit across 10 dimensions. The billing system is production-grade with strong infrastructure (Redis locking, webhook replay prevention, 3-stage dunning, lifecycle tracking, PQL system, customer health scoring). Key gaps: no tax implementation, GDPR billing data export missing, no multi-currency, template-only legal content, no A/B pricing experiments beyond Pro tier variant, no referral/affiliate program, EXPANSION lifecycle stage not auto-triggered.

findings.title: 5-tier pricing hierarchy well-structured
findings.description: Tier hierarchy (free → pro → pro_team → team → enterprise) defined in config/plans.php with environment-driven pricing. All prices configurable via env vars with sensible defaults.

findings.title: Annual discount consistently 20% across tiers
findings.description: Pro: $19/mo → $182/yr (20% off). Team: $49/seat/mo → $470/seat/yr (20% off). Pro Team: $39/seat/mo → $374/seat/yr (20% off). Consistent discount messaging.

findings.title: Pro tier A/B price variant infrastructure exists but limited
findings.description: PLAN_PRO_PRICE_MONTHLY_VARIANT env var exists for 50% cohort testing via CRC32 hash of user/session ID. Only Pro monthly is testable; no infrastructure for testing annual prices, other tiers, or feature bundles.
findings.recommendation: Extend A/B testing to cover annual pricing, other tiers, and feature positioning. Consider a more robust experimentation framework.

findings.title: Enterprise pricing is fully custom with no anchor
findings.description: Enterprise tier shows no price at all (null monthly/annual). No 'starting at' anchor price to set expectations before the sales conversation.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a 'starting at $X/seat/mo' anchor or 'Custom — typically $99-199/seat' range to qualify leads before they contact sales.

findings.title: Per-seat pricing with enforced min/max
findings.description: Pro Team: 2-4 seats. Team: 2-50 seats. Enterprise: 10+ seats. Validated at subscription creation and quantity update.

findings.title: Plan limits well-differentiated across tiers
findings.description: Free: 1 project/50 items/1 token/7d history. Pro: unlimited projects+items/10 tokens/365d. Team: +25 tokens/unlimited history. Enterprise: unlimited everything.

findings.title: Pro Team tier creates positioning ambiguity
findings.description: 5 tiers (free/pro/pro_team/team/enterprise) may confuse buyers. Pro Team ($39/seat, 2-4 seats) overlaps significantly with Team ($49/seat, 2-50 seats) — same limits except max seats and API tokens (10 vs 25). The $10/seat premium for Team only adds seats 5-50, audit log, and unlimited history.
findings.recommendation: Consider merging Pro Team into Team with a lower entry point, or sharply differentiating features. 5 tiers is atypical — most SaaS succeeds with 3-4.

findings.title: Feature list strings are marketing copy embedded in config
findings.description: Plan features are hardcoded string arrays in config/plans.php. No structured feature matrix (boolean per-feature-per-tier) for programmatic comparison or dynamic rendering.
findings.recommendation: Consider a structured feature matrix for automated plan comparison tables and upgrade nudges.

findings.title: Coming soon flag enables soft-launch
findings.description: PRO_TIER_COMING_SOON env var disables checkout for individual tiers while keeping them visible on the pricing page.

findings.title: Free tier limits very restrictive — potential friction
findings.description: Free tier allows only 1 project and 7-day history. This may be too restrictive to demonstrate value before conversion, especially for evaluation-stage users.
findings.recommendation: Consider 2-3 projects and 30-day history for free tier to reduce friction while maintaining upgrade motivation.

findings.title: Dual checkout paths: Stripe Checkout + direct subscription
findings.description: Both Stripe hosted Checkout (redirect) and direct subscription creation (in-app) supported. Stripe Checkout handles PCI, SCA, and payment UI. Direct path available for payment method on file.

findings.title: Coupon support throughout checkout flow
findings.description: Coupon codes accepted via URL parameter (?coupon=) and manual entry. Validated against Stripe API before checkout. Applied to both new subscriptions and plan swaps.

findings.title: 14-day platform trial with pro-tier access
findings.description: Trial grants pro-tier limits for 14 days. Trial-to-paid conversion tracked. Trial ending reminders sent 3 days before expiry.

findings.title: No checkout abandonment recovery
findings.description: If a user starts Stripe Checkout but doesn't complete payment, there's no abandoned cart email or follow-up. Only incomplete (SCA) subscriptions get reminders.
findings.recommendation: Implement Stripe Checkout session expiry webhook (checkout.session.expired) to trigger abandonment recovery emails.

findings.title: Comprehensive analytics event tracking on checkout funnel
findings.description: Events tracked: PRICING_VIEWED, PLAN_SELECTED, CHECKOUT_STARTED, CHECKOUT_COMPLETED, PERIOD_TOGGLED. Enables full funnel analysis.

findings.title: Rate limiting on checkout may frustrate legitimate retries
findings.description: Checkout and subscribe endpoints throttled at 5/minute. Users who encounter payment issues and need to retry quickly could hit this limit.
findings.recommendation: Consider increasing to 10/minute or implementing smarter throttling that allows retries after payment failures.

findings.title: Complete subscription state machine implemented
findings.description: All Stripe subscription states handled: active, trialing, past_due, canceled, incomplete, incomplete_expired. Grace period logic, trial management, and state transitions fully tested with 21 billing test files.

findings.title: 7-stage lifecycle tracking with history
findings.description: VISITOR → TRIAL → ACTIVATED → PAYING → EXPANSION → AT_RISK → CHURNED. All transitions recorded in user_stage_history with metadata and days-in-stage.

findings.title: Redis lock concurrency protection on all mutations
findings.description: 35-second Redis locks on create, cancel, resume, swap, and quantity operations. Database-level unique constraint prevents duplicate active subscriptions.

findings.title: Webhook out-of-order protection
findings.description: last_webhook_at timestamp tracking prevents processing stale webhook events. Older events acknowledged (200) but not processed.

findings.title: EXPANSION lifecycle stage not auto-triggered
findings.description: The EXPANSION stage exists in the enum but no automatic transition is triggered when users upgrade plans or increase seats. Stage must be set manually.
findings.recommendation: Auto-transition to EXPANSION when swapping to a higher tier or increasing quantity beyond initial purchase.

findings.title: Account deletion handles active subscriptions
findings.description: Active subscriptions canceled immediately on account deletion. Orphaned subscription cleanup job dispatched if cancellation fails. Grace period subscriptions handled.

findings.title: 3-stage dunning email campaign
findings.description: Email 1 at day 3-5 (gentle), email 2 at day 7-10 (urgent), email 3 at day 12-15 (final notice). Deduplication via email_send_logs. Plan name included in emails.

findings.title: 7-day past-due grace period before access revocation
findings.description: Users retain paid-tier access for 7 days (configurable) after payment failure. Past-due timestamp tracked for precise timing.

findings.title: Incomplete payment (SCA/3DS) recovery
findings.description: Reminders at 1-hour and 12-hour marks for incomplete subscriptions. 23-hour total window. PaymentActionRequiredNotification with hosted invoice URL.

findings.title: Payment recovery notification clears dunning
findings.description: When payment succeeds after past_due, past_due_since is cleared and PaymentRecoveredNotification sent. Dunning sequence stops automatically.

findings.title: Involuntary churn distinguished from voluntary
findings.description: Webhook handler distinguishes payment_failed churn from user-initiated cancellation. Involuntary churn triggers sympathetic win-back notification with 3-day delay.

findings.title: Retention coupon offer during cancellation
findings.description: 20% discount for 3 months offered when user selects 'too_expensive' as cancellation reason. Coupon ID configurable via RETENTION_COUPON_ID env var.

findings.title: 3-email win-back sequence for voluntary churn
findings.description: Email 1 at day 3-5 (feedback), email 2 at day 14-17 (new features), email 3 at day 30-33 (final offer). Respects marketing opt-out. Skips reactivated users.

findings.title: Cancellation reason and feedback capture
findings.description: 5 cancellation reasons (too_expensive, switching_tools, no_longer_needed, missing_features, other) with optional 500-char feedback. Stored in audit logs.

findings.title: Customer health scoring system
findings.description: 0-100 health score based on login frequency (25pts), feature adoption (25pts), billing status (25pts), profile completion (25pts). Brackets: Critical/At-Risk/Moderate/Healthy.

findings.title: No proactive retention intervention for at-risk customers
findings.description: Customer health scoring exists but no automated outreach when health score drops below threshold. AT_RISK lifecycle stage is only triggered by payment failure, not low engagement.
findings.recommendation: Add automated engagement-based risk detection: if health score drops below 25 (Critical) for 7+ days, trigger a re-engagement email or in-app prompt.

findings.title: Retention coupon only offered for 'too_expensive' reason
findings.description: The 20% retention discount is only surfaced when the user selects 'too_expensive'. Users selecting 'missing_features' or 'switching_tools' see a generic 'Talk to us' link instead of a targeted offer.
findings.recommendation: Consider offering a personalized retention offer for other reasons (e.g., roadmap preview for missing_features, feature comparison for switching_tools).

findings.title: PQL (Product Qualified Lead) system with threshold nudges
findings.description: Usage monitoring at 50%, 80%, 100% thresholds. 80% and 100% trigger upgrade nudge notifications. 14-day rate limiting per threshold. In-app upgrade prompt when limits hit.

findings.title: Seat expansion flow for team plans
findings.description: UpdateQuantity endpoint allows seat additions within tier limits. SCA handling for price increases. Analytics event tracking.

findings.title: No referral or affiliate program
findings.description: No referral tracking, affiliate codes, or word-of-mouth incentive system exists. This is a significant growth channel gap for a SaaS template product.
findings.recommendation: Implement a referral program with credit-based rewards (e.g., 1 month free for referrer and referee). Can be simple: unique referral codes stored on users table, tracked at registration.

findings.title: No usage-based or metered billing
findings.description: All pricing is fixed monthly/annual or per-seat. No overage charges, metered events, or consumption-based pricing. Stripe meter infrastructure exists in migrations but unused.
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing metered billing for API tokens or data volume as a growth lever. Stripe meter_id and meter_event_name columns already exist.

findings.title: No in-app upgrade path from billing dashboard
findings.description: The billing dashboard shows subscription status but the primary upgrade path is via the pricing page. No inline tier comparison or one-click upgrade from the billing view.
findings.recommendation: Add an inline plan comparison card or 'Upgrade to X' quick action on the billing dashboard for users on lower tiers.

findings.title: Expansion nudge notification exists
findings.description: ExpansionNudgeNotification targets users in EXPANSION stage with 'See Team Plans' CTA. However, EXPANSION stage is not auto-triggered (see SL-005).

findings.title: Buy page has strong value anchoring
findings.description: Build vs Buy comparison table shows 200-400 hours / $15,000-$30,000 cost to build from scratch. Good PMF section with 'good fit if' / 'not for you if' qualification.

findings.title: Comprehensive billing metrics dashboard
findings.description: Admin dashboard tracks: MRR, active subscriptions, churn rate, trial conversion, activation rate, signup-to-paid conversion, cohort retention (1/2/4/8 weeks), tier distribution, voluntary vs involuntary churn breakdown.

findings.title: Lifecycle stage funnel visualization
findings.description: Admin dashboard shows user counts per lifecycle stage in funnel order. Stage velocity (median days between transitions) calculated from 90-day window.

findings.title: Product analytics service tracks onboarding and feature adoption
findings.description: Onboarding funnel (register → started → completed), signup trends, feature adoption (top 10), subscription events (created/canceled/resumed) all tracked.

findings.title: No ARPU or LTV calculation
findings.description: MRR and churn rate are calculated but Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) and Customer Lifetime Value (LTV) are not. These are critical SaaS metrics for pricing decisions.
findings.recommendation: Add ARPU (MRR / active subscribers) and estimated LTV (ARPU / monthly churn rate) to the admin billing dashboard.

findings.title: No revenue cohort analysis
findings.description: User retention cohorts exist (1/2/4/8 week) but no revenue retention cohorts. Can't track dollar retention (net revenue retention > 100% indicates expansion).
findings.recommendation: Add net revenue retention (NRR) cohort analysis: track MRR from a cohort over time to measure expansion vs contraction revenue.

findings.title: 12 distinct billing analytics events tracked on frontend
findings.description: Full funnel coverage: pricing viewed, plan selected, checkout started/completed, subscription canceled/resumed, plan swapped, trial upgrade clicked, payment failed, period toggled, payment method updated.

findings.title: Tax integration not implemented despite feature flag
findings.description: FEATURE_BILLING_TAX env var and config exist but Stripe Tax is not integrated into subscription creation or checkout. No automatic_tax parameter passed to Stripe.
findings.recommendation: Implement Stripe Tax integration before launching in jurisdictions requiring tax collection. Add automatic_tax: {enabled: true} to checkout sessions and subscription creation when flag is enabled.

findings.title: Legal content is placeholder only
findings.description: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy pages contain explicit 'Do Not Use As-Is' disclaimer. Template content only. No agreement version tracking or acceptance timestamps.
findings.recommendation: Replace with reviewed legal agreements. Implement agreement version tracking with acceptance timestamps in the database. Record acceptance at registration and on TOS changes.

findings.title: GDPR data export excludes billing data
findings.description: PersonalDataExportController exports user profile, settings, social accounts, tokens, webhooks, and audit logs — but NOT subscription history, payment methods, invoices, refund history, or Stripe customer ID.
findings.recommendation: Extend data export to include: subscription history (type, status, dates), invoice list with amounts, refund records, Stripe customer ID, and payment method metadata (type + last 4 only).

findings.title: No billing address collection
findings.description: No billing address fields exist in the database or checkout flow. Required for tax compliance in many jurisdictions and proper invoice generation.
findings.recommendation: Add billing address collection to checkout flow. Store in customers table or pass to Stripe for tax calculation. Required for EU VAT, US state sales tax.

findings.title: No VAT/GST number collection or validation
findings.description: No VAT ID field, no reverse charge mechanism for B2B EU transactions, no GST handling for Australian/Indian customers.
findings.recommendation: Add VAT ID collection for EU B2B customers. Stripe Tax handles validation and reverse charge automatically when enabled.

findings.title: PCI compliance properly delegated to Stripe
findings.description: No card data stored in application. Only pm_type and pm_last_four stored. All payment processing via Stripe hosted checkout or Payment Methods API.

findings.title: PII properly blocked from analytics
findings.description: AnalyticsGateway blocklist prevents billing_address, credit_card, cvv, account_number from reaching GA4.

findings.title: Single currency per deployment
findings.description: Currency configured via CASHIER_CURRENCY (default USD). formatCurrency() helper supports locale-aware formatting but no multi-currency pricing or currency detection per user.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for US-focused launch. For international expansion, implement Stripe's multi-currency pricing or use Stripe Checkout's automatic currency conversion.

findings.title: Redis-locked concurrent operation prevention
findings.description: All subscription mutations (create, cancel, resume, swap, quantity) protected by 35-second Redis locks. ConcurrentOperationException thrown on lock failure with 409 response.

findings.title: Database-level unique constraint on active subscriptions
findings.description: Unique constraint on (user_id, type) WHERE ends_at IS NULL prevents duplicate active subscriptions at the database level. Driver-specific implementations for SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL.

findings.title: Webhook signature verification with timestamp validation
findings.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature verification on all webhooks. 300-second timestamp tolerance for replay prevention. Out-of-order event detection via last_webhook_at.

findings.title: Invoice URL sanitization prevents SSRF
findings.description: Invoice PDF URLs validated for HTTPS scheme and stripe.com domain. Prevents injection of malicious URLs through Stripe response manipulation.

findings.title: Rate limiting on all billing endpoints
findings.description: Checkout/subscribe: 5/min. Retention coupon: 3/60min. Webhook: 120/min. Billing portal: standard auth rate limits.

findings.title: SCA/3DS Strong Customer Authentication fully implemented
findings.description: IncompletePayment exception handling on checkout, swap, and quantity update. PaymentActionRequiredNotification with hosted invoice URL. Incomplete payment reminders at 1h and 12h.

findings.title: Webhook secret enforced in non-local environments
findings.description: StripeWebhookController constructor throws RuntimeException if STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is missing in non-local environments.

findings.title: CSP and security headers protect billing pages
findings.description: X-Frame-Options: DENY prevents clickjacking. form-action: 'self' restricts form submissions. HSTS in production. Nonce-based CSP for scripts.

cross_cutting_findings.title: No checkout abandonment recovery
cross_cutting_findings.description: Stripe Checkout session abandonment is not tracked or recovered. Only SCA-incomplete subscriptions get follow-up. Lost revenue from users who click 'Upgrade' but don't complete payment.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Implement checkout.session.expired webhook handler to trigger abandonment recovery email with deep link back to pricing page with pre-selected plan.

cross_cutting_findings.title: EXPANSION lifecycle stage is dead code
cross_cutting_findings.description: LifecycleStage::EXPANSION exists in enum and ExpansionNudgeNotification targets it, but no code path auto-transitions users to this stage. Plan upgrades transition to PAYING, not EXPANSION.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Pre-launch blockers in tax and legal
cross_cutting_findings.description: Three items must be resolved before production launch: (1) Replace placeholder legal content, (2) Implement billing data in GDPR export, (3) Enable tax collection for applicable jurisdictions.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Health scoring not connected to lifecycle transitions
cross_cutting_findings.description: CustomerHealthService calculates 0-100 scores but doesn't trigger AT_RISK for low-engagement users. AT_RISK is only triggered by payment failure, missing engagement-based risk detection.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Missing ARPU and LTV metrics for pricing optimization
cross_cutting_findings.description: MRR and churn rate exist but ARPU and LTV are not calculated. These metrics are essential for evaluating whether current pricing captures sufficient value.

strengths: Production-grade subscription lifecycle with Redis locking and database-level uniqueness
Comprehensive retention system: cancellation reasons, retention coupons, 3-email win-back
PQL system with usage threshold nudges at 50/80/100%
Customer health scoring (login + adoption + billing + profile)
Full billing analytics: MRR, churn, trial conversion, cohort retention, stage funnel
Security: webhook replay prevention, SCA/3DS, invoice URL sanitization, CSP
critical_gaps: Tax integration not implemented (feature flag exists but unused)
Legal content is placeholder — no agreement tracking
GDPR data export excludes billing data
EXPANSION lifecycle stage is orphaned (no auto-trigger)
